name: PAPILLARY ENDOLYMPHATIC SAC TUMOR
definition: HEFFNER TUMOR locally invasive papillary cystadenomatous tumor of temporal bone

demographics.Associated with: von Hippel-Lindau disease (in 7%), which may have bilateral papillary

discriminating_features: moth-eaten lytic appearance of invaded bone surrounded by thin shell of reactive bone:
intratumoral calcifications = "bone sequestra" from destruction of petrous bone
may be hypervascular (supplied by branches of external carotid artery)
speckled pattern of hyperintensity on T1WI (mimicking glomus tumor)

CT: moth-eaten lytic appearance of invaded bone surrounded by thin shell of reactive bone:
intratumoral calcifications = "bone sequestra" from destruction of petrous bone
may be hypervascular (supplied by branches of external carotid artery)
MR: speckled pattern of hyperintensity on T1WI (mimicking glomus tumor)
commonly contains blood products (hyperintense on T1WI + hypointense on T2WI)
areas of low SI may represent hemosiderin
